reviews.review_description: I’ve been playing Shadow Point.  Now the game won’t load, it’s frozen at the book and won’t open.  I’ve uninstalled and reinstalled doesn’t work.  Powered off and on, still doesn’t load.  I’ve contacted the developer, no answer!  help!!!

reviews.review_title: How have I never heard of this game!
reviews.review_description: An incredible game! A beautifully crafted world, an emotive story and incredible atmospheric music accompanied by amazing voice acting. The puzzles start off simple, with basic shadow manipulation but as the game progresses new mind bending mechanics appear! A must play for any puzzle game lover. I recommend you play seated, as for even people who don’t normally feel sick in vr, the mindbending game design can leave you feeling a little queasy! Can you save Lorna McCabe or will she remain trapped in the world beyond Shadow Point Observatory forever?

This game is awesome! Although I had a little bit of a hard time understanding the story (though that’s probably just me lol), I absolutely loved these puzzles. Every single puzzle is the same on the surface, but they are executed in different, mind-bending ways: mirrors that let you hand yourself different objects than you came in with, crystals that open doors when lit up, objects that physically change other objects, portals that change both the gravity of you and other objects, windows of time, and many other incredible elements. Wrapped up with a great OST, sound design, scenery and voice acting, I would definitely recommend this game!

I'm disappointed because this looked like a great game but the puzzles are the same but just a bit harder as you go up levels. I lost interest after a few levels because theres nothing new to work on. I gave an extra star because the graphics and story line are exciting.
I threw the apple in the well or fountain, and can't go any further, very disappointed.

Stuck near the beginning. It's impossible to pick up the key from the well. Here I am hopping around, fiddling with that magnifying glass looking thing trying to solve a possible puzzle. Finally decided to cheat and look it up online. Only to find out you're supposed to just pick it up. Wish I could get my money back.

I picked this up primarily because it was only £1.99 in the Christmas sale and the other reviews. For that insanely low price, it was an absolute steal, but even at full price, I wouldn't have felt disappointed having now completed the game.

Firstly, don't be put off by the graphics. This was released in 2019, so it was originally intended for the Oculus Quest. Yes, they may appear a bit basic compared to Quest 3 optimised games, but they definitely grew on me. They're perfectly functional, bright and colourful and towards the end, I really liked the art style.

If you're a fan of a decent puzzle game, you really have to add this to your list. At first, I thought this is really simple as you're introduced to some of the basic mechanics which primarily involves light and shadow, hence the name of game. Don't be put off thinking the game will be a breeze. Gradually, more mechanics are added such as mirrors, gravity warps, inversion, different perspectives that alter time, etc. By midway, there were certain puzzle elements that became really challenging and you really have to survey your entire surroundings and plan ahead, noting where you'll appear next through a portal in a world reminiscent of an M C Escher artwork. Some of the puzzles, particularly the very last one I encountered are really well designed and cleverly done. It makes me wonder how the devs came up with the ideas. I've played through puzzlers such Red Matter 1 & 2, Ghost Town, Infinity Indside and although graphically they're far superior, this game trumps them in some respects at times with the design.

On top of the puzzles, you have a great story, an emotional impact and narration by Patrick Stewart! Even at the every end credits, you have a great song and I just felt that it was a strong, satisfying conclusion. The only reason I can't give 5 stars was there was a minor bug involving dipping your head into a water fountain to pass through a portal that took many attempts. I very nearly gave up and thought I was doing something wrong. I have read another user report on this as well. As you try to maneuver into position, the game keeps telling you to return to the playing area. I also found myself having to contort both arms and wrists at times with certain puzzles to cast the correct shadow on a wall to progress. Again, I started to think that I was doing something wrong. I eventually cracked it but I can see some folks giving up entirely. I'm able bodied and fit. Some players won't be and confined to wheel chairs so I can see some parts being almost impossible for them which is a shame. 

There was also one puzzle that was nigh on impossible. I managed to do it but I'm not entirely sure if it was even the right solution and I hadn't just managed to bend the game mechanics. It was one of optional extra "moon puzzles." In other words, you can still complete the game without it. However, you need to solve all puzzles to unlock another bonus mini world, "The garden."

In essence, if you're a fan of VR puzzle games, definitely give this a try. With some patience and perseverance, you'll be rewarded.
Decent game.  Recommended.  Some of the puzzles are tough.

I’ve played some games with puzzles, but so far, this one takes the cake. They were challenging, but with enough thinking I could get through. My favorite part though was the story and the mystery. There are games that can get me invested, but none have done it like this one. Now I’m trying to figure out how to do the story justice whenever I share how awesome this thing is. I want more games like this please!

A cute yet mysterious world has been created for the player. The puzzles are interesting and fully doable without using the hint option.
Playing seated does unfortunately hinder picking up certain items(the key in the well early on) this must be fixed.
Other than that, I do love the story, music and characters.

It’s refreshing to have a game where you can explore without even trying to solve the puzzles and it’s just as entertaining as the puzzles themselves. I smile just looking around! The puzzles and worlds are different enough in each level it’s not boring yet you learn how to do new things along the way to help you later in the game. It seemed to have so many worlds I want to play it again as I can’t return all!

Awesome game. As I got closer to the end I started over to follow the storyline I hadn’t been paying enough attention to. It is a big part of the game and you don’t want to miss it. The puzzles start out simplistic but quickly escalate playing with dimensions. It can be a lot to wrap your head around and I did turn to YouTube walkthroughs for some help (nod to Dave the Psycho).  Worth it to get it played through .  Loved Ghost Town, The Room and 7th Guest. This tops them for me. More encompassing somehow.

reviews.review_title: Fun at first…but then just frustrating!
reviews.review_description: I have had this game for a couple of years. I really enjoy puzzle games and loved The Room so much that I purchased several like it. This one being among them. At first it was a lot of fun. The puzzles, story, and world was unique and interesting. However it got more and more challenging to the point that it became frustrating and less enjoyable to play. Then in the final chapters I continually got a “Return to play area” error and had to keep restarting the level over. I finally gave up and just watched a gameplay video online to see how it ended. All in all it would have been a great game if the puzzles remained solvable and the game could be played without errors every 10 minutes at the end.

Has this been on my wishlist for a long time. Decided to finally buy it after a discount. It's an interesting puzzle game, it get increasingly harder and introduces portal and gravity mechanics in addition to the shadow puzzles. Finished it in a couple of days of playing. Some might get frustrated with the later puzzles, but I was able to complete it. I enjoyed it a lot.
I enjoyed my time solving the puzzles in Shadow Point. Bonus: Patrick Stewart.

Update!  My original issue has been resolved and the game is 👍 
I purchased the game a while back and the sound did not work at all so I returned it.  After some time and curiosity I purchased it again thinking it was fixed the problem continues.  I read developer responses  from before and this was supposed to be ironed out.  
Come on devs I really want to experience this game.
Am I missing something?  Is it completely silent through tutorial and up to shadow point?  
Frustrated because it is my type game.  
Please help!

I picked this one up because of Patrick Steward, but the game itself is so captivating you'll quickly forget about him. The graphics are simple, but colorful and sufficient. The puzzles are interesting and progressively more difficult. I really liked doing them and never had to look anything up. One hint: sometimes you need to stick your head in something ;-)

Story is good.. even a little emotional at times.

This is an excellent game. Vr done right!
This is a fun game. It will take some mind thought. "shadow" is the main word here. It will test you but it is really fun.
It´s a very good puzzle game with a more than decent story

This game is amazing. I love the art style, the puzzles, the story. Everything about it is lovely. However, through no fault of the devs, I'm stuck. I can't lean down far enough to grab the key on the Huntress level. One of the perils of being a wheelchair user. I'll keep trying at it, because I want to see where this game goes.

reviews.review_title: Game keeps crashing on last level 😭
reviews.review_description: This game was actually really interesting and had some challenging moments. The story is also good. But now I can’t finish the game because it keeps crashing on the last level. I’m beyond frustrated 😩 I just want to finish the game. I don’t know if this has to do with the horizon os or the actual game. I have to assume since it’s been fine with every other level there’s something wrong with the game. I’ve rebooted the system. Deleted the game and reinstalled it. Tried all the usual suspects. Nothing seems to fix it
Quest 3 deserves graphics for ur splendid game

Shadowpoint is excellent. It is very rare for me to even consider a max rating but this game deserves every single star. My one criticism is that because this game is quite old it hasn't received any resolution uplifts to match the power of the Quest 2/3. This is the first game I've felt compelled to use Quest Games Optimiser to crank up that resolution and once I did oh what a difference did it make. 

What starts as a deceptively simple puzzle mechanic: making shadows from objects to solve picture puzzles, branches out further into some mind-bending, awe-striking puzzle-solving from gravity shenanigans to interfacing with parallel universes. A real highlight for me was the introduction of a portable window into a different version of the same level. The moment I peered into this thing while moving it around with my hand to see the other world is a gaming moment I'll remember forever. 

The visuals are simple but they are beautiful, opting for a cartoonish, hand-painted vibrant world making every level a joy to experience. While technically the graphical fidelity is very low, it doesn't really matter because the art design is so strong and heavily stylized. It reminded me a lot old 3D platformers, particularly Spyro with the vibrant colours and dragon motifs, so that was a nice nostalgia hit.

The story was intriguing. While nothing too crazy it kept my interest the whole time and by the end of the game left me with a satisfied smile and little emotional. The voice work is excellent, which you'd expect nothing less of Patrick Stewart.

This game is a poster child for what VR can do, as the shadowpuppet mechanic could only ever be a game with fine motion controls and that's part of what I love about this game. It's a VR game through and through, everything designed from the ground-up with the platform and it's limitations and capabilities in mind. It's utterly perfect.
There are obvious bugs in the game that the vendor doesn't fix.  Don't waste your time.

reviews.review_title: The Best Game I've Ever Played
reviews.review_description: I'm constantly searching for a game that comes close to this. It's a game that you miss and feel sad when it's over. Get it!

I liked the basic idea of the game and quite enjoyed it to start with. A few things became frustrating, firstly the problem with being outside the play area, this was a bit annoying, why didn't it just teleport me back in? Secondly, the sliding movement, I found this quite unpleasant and had to use the teleporting which I didn't really want to use. Thirdly, this was the biggest issue for me, when trying to fit the shapes it required them to be so precisely fitted. I found this not just frustrating but eventually became painful, my right hand and wrist were under a lot of strain with the need to keep pressure on the trigger for so long and often in very contorted ways. Even worse some puzzles required me to do this with both hands. I got half way through chapter two and had to give up, my hand was too sore to continue and my thumb was twitching weirdly for hours, not good.

Really good.  First person story-driven puzzle adventure.  Really enjoyable, and well paced.  

I played with hints off and any time I got stuck, I could always figure things out before I got frustrated.  The puzzles are often very cerebral, so if you're not used to puzzles like that, you might struggle.  But there is a hint system to help you, but I can't say anything about it because I never tried it.

I found one bug where I was able to grab something in a cage  when I shouldn't have; very minor.

reviews.review_title: Great game if it would work
reviews.review_description: This is a really enjoyable and fun game. Me and my mum play this together through chatting. But almost every time we go to try to continue the game one of us always ends up losing our save data and having to start all over again and thus has happened several times to both of us. We have both tried different techniques to try to not lose the save data but it hasn't worked. They need to fix this otherwise what's the point of playing a game you can never get the whole way through unless you sit and play the whole thing in one sitting. It's sad because it is a good game but I hope you can fix it because it makes it frustrating and not enjoyable because of this. I've seen many others also complain of this issue and had no help or feedback. So please please can they hear us and deal with it on behalf of all of us struggling. God bless

Really good game enjoyed it so much I bought it for my daughter so we could do it together.  Only problem we’ve both had is that we get through the levels and when we come back to the game we’ve lost all our progress and have had to start again several times. Have brought this up to Meta who advised contacting the developer. I contacted the developer only to find that other people had reported it too and the developers are not doing anything about it. They haven’t even replied.  Such a shame coz it could be a really good game. Hence the mark down. Coz now we’re getting really frustrated. Meta should put pressure on the developers or remove the game.

SUMMARY:
Interesting environments that you need to wander to find disparate pieces (objects) then work out how to solve the various puzzles. Unfortunately I didn't the story or the dialogue interesting.

THE GOOD:
The difficulty of the puzzles ramped up nicely and I found them interesting once you passed the first several that showed the basic mechanics of the shadow puzzles. The official Youtube walkthrough is ordered nicely and succinct, and this was very helpful and quick solving in those times when I got stuck.

COULD BE BETTER:
I didn't find the story compelling. I got the gist of it, but I found the story telling disjointed, as it's all conveyed verbally via the voice over (mainly when you start each chapter) and the (only) character in the game.  The story is fantasy non logic stuff, and unfortunately, that had a flow on effect too for the dialogue. It was just uninteresting and meaningless really. The only benefit I found is that the voice over and character negated wandering around empty environments by yourself.

THE BAD:
I was unable to finish the game as it would crash each time several minutes after I started The Garden chapter. I was close to the end so just watched the official walkthrough of the remaining chapters to get closure.

developer_response.body:
Hello!
We're really sorry to hear you had a frustrating experience - could you please provide any more detail about the crash you faced so we can have our team investigate?

reviews.review_description: I love puzzle games, and I was excited to try this one! I loved it all the way up to when I had to reach into a well to get an apple. It won’t let me! I can’t go further in the game until I figure this out! Devs please help!

reviews.review_title: Love the game but can't go further
reviews.review_description: For some reason I can't reach in the well and grab the apple I've tried changing my boundary and seated vs standing. Nothing will fix it really disappointed.

developer_response.body: Hi potato. We're sorry you're having some difficulty here. The team are aware of it and working on a solution. Which version of Meta are you playing on please?

reviews.review_description: Great game, overall rather fun, the next to last stage has some issues which cause the game to crash to quest menu, restarting the device a few times fixed it long enough for me to finish the stage, otherwise a great game

This is a bit of a non-sensical game, pretty unsatisfying, background music can lead to early madness, then to cap it off, I got caught in a glitch on chapter 2. Really not worth trying to unglitch it. Time to delete and write it off.

reviews.review_title: Good game, with one giant problem
reviews.review_description:
First: the pros. The puzzles are really fun. They're all solvable logically, even if it doesn't look like it at first.

The story is compelling. You want to finish it and see where it goes.

The graphics are excellent. No issues there.

The con: If you move out of bounds, the game black screens you. Even following the arrow, I usually can't get back to the game and have to completely back out and start over. This is not only frustrating, I have motion sickness, and having to walk means I can't play for long. 

All in all, would recommend if you don't mind walking.

The puzzles in this were really inventive, reminding me of Portal, Braid, and other great puzzle games.  Meanwhile, the world and the story got me hooked once it came into focus, and I had to keep going to see how the story would end.  The game has mechanics that wouldn't work nearly as well if it wasn't in VR, really taking advantage of the player being able to move their hands freely in a 3D space.  Highly recommended!
They should give this , moss , and super hot to every new owner. Or at least 1 show these newbs the best of vr then they'll be hooked. And then re4 and GTA next they will really see potential

I enjoy the game but wanted to suggest they change how the game loads. It’s blinding white. It would be better to be dim. It hurts the eyes. I have to close mine but it’s still bright when I do that. Then the game comes on & it’s darker. Eyes then have to adjust. Think this could be valuable change for players. Other than that it’s been a enjoyable game. I do get a little motion sick from the moving place to place but that’s more tolerable than the blinding white light at start up & in between game levels.

reviews.review_title: Don't get discouraged if this happens...
reviews.review_description: I have plenty of fitness games, and I don't enjoy shooter games. I was looking for something different and picked Shadow Point after much research. The game is delightful, enchanting, engaging and beautifully done. I didn't want to try to finish the game in one sitting  - but initially my progress was not being saved even though I exited the game gracefully (using the game's menu button, returning to main menu, quitting game). If this happens to you, please TRY IT AGAIN! After losing my progress two times, I was able to continue playing at my leisure for several weeks. I thoroughly enjoyed this game, and it rewarded me with hours of entertainment. Challenging enough to keep it interesting, but not difficult enough to be frustrating. The story line, and first class voice acting, really made me felt connected to the journey. The team behind this game has created an absolute gem.

The story comes together very well at the end, especially if you complete it 100%. No spoilers, just go for all achievements! The puzzles get progressively more challenging as you go along, keeping it fun the entire time without being too repetitive. Great game!

reviews.review_title: Can’t get out of the tutorial
reviews.review_description: I can’t get out of the tutorial because I can’t seem to move. The directions say use the left joystick which I try and nothing happens. I’ve uninstalled and reinstalled and still, can’t move. Am I missing something? I’d hate to refund this game because Patrick Stewart gives me life…

I hate this game. Let me say it one more time for those people in the back. I hate it. It’s supposed to challenge you but it doesn’t it’s absolutely impossible to maneuver through it unless you refer to a cheat or walkthrough. And I don’t want to cheat I want to have fun. Please save your money and buy anything else. Unless you enjoy being aggravated and unsatisfied.  Rating it was one star was a gift. But there’s no option for no stars.  There’s also a tendency for motion sickness so beware. I couldn’t get my money back but hopefully I’ll save you from losing yours.
Cool puzzles, bad storytelling, buggy
It's just ok. Definately 1st gen quality.
Title says it all. If you like puzzle games with an interesting story... buy it!

Shadow Point rests in the shadow of the other puzzle games I purchased first, no pun intended. Therefore, it's difficult to give it a fair rating. I would say Shadow Point is a 3.5 * in comparison. 

Here are the ratings of games I've completed thus far:  Moss - 5 * ; The Room VR: A Dark Matter - 4.5 * ; Red Matter - 3 * 

Shadow Point started off extremely slow and somewhat childish. I was thinking I made a mistake, and that this game was geared more towards children. I remember saying this is a 1 * and then I played a little further, make that 2 *. As I progressed more and more, make that 2.5, no 3, and then I ended with 3.5 stars in comparison. Although, if I hadn't seen what the Oculus Quest 2 is capable of with Moss and The Room VR: A Dark Matter, I would have given it at least 4 stars.  

Shadow Point became more intriguing after the new chapters were revealed. The concept of using mirrors and spatial orientation to solve puzzles was ingenious, so hats off to the developers. The one area where it beats all the other games I mentioned is the game time. Shadow Point is at least twice as long, and just when I thought it was almost over, it continued on and on. 

The few gripes I had with the game are 1. The sound effects and music could be annoying at times, and 2. why do they have to make everything so sad and depressing? 3. The graphics were not all that good, again compared.  4. I also would have enjoyed a more upbeat story. It seems that every puzzle game, except for maybe Moss, is dark and gloomy. 

All in all I would recommend this for those who like to use their wit to solve puzzles. You will be challenged and the reward is fulfilling.

Another VR campaign game that only has one player profile available to save game progress on. This is just a silly thing to do. "The Room VR: A Dark Matter" has three profiles to use to save game play. Is it that big of a deal? Great game if you don't share your quest with a SO or family (kids). Otherwise you, or they, will have to wait until somebody has completed the game in it's entirety before they can begin their own game. Oculus should specifically list the number of player profiles supported in the game details, "Game Mode" doesn't cover it.
Crashes every 15 minutes!!! Glitches throughout!!! Don't waste your time or money!!
My wife and I had hours of fun with this game! If you like solving puzzles, this is the game for you!

An absolute joy to experience from start to end. Fantastic level design with a perfect learning curve, so many different puzzle variations to keep things fresh and keep you on your toes, a deep and mind bending story that constantly made me want to push forward and a beautiful art style to accompany it along the way. Coatsink really nailed this one, and I am so excited to see what comes next from them! Cannot recommend this enough for puzzle fans or just anyone looking a neat VR experience; even novice puzzle players will have an amazing time because it does such a great job teaching you the mechanics and never fails to make you feel smart! 😊

From beginning it is pretty interesting show. But later after few passages its start to be bit boring and you need to over a over solve similar kind of puzzle. Extra moon puzzles are great.
Game is buggy when you moving trough portals and also some parts are unfinishable in sitting position despite that you choose sitting control.
My game has no sound. I've checked my settings, other games work fine, but this one is just silent. If I didn't turn the subtitles on I would have had no idea what was even going on.
Good and challenging game

Great game, if you like room escapes and puzzlers this is a wonderful one. Take that to mean everything is great. The below are my nitpicky things to be aware of:

Story is fine, but a bit ambiguous and could be more developed.

Some of the mechanics of working puzzles could be a bit less “particular” (you rely on making shadows to solve puzzles) as I found myself struggling in circumstances to get it just perfect for the game to accept it.
The saves sometimes disappears... And then you have to start again...

This game was nothing short of an absolute delight. 
* The mechanics are clever. 
* The gameplay is balanced, challenging, rewarding, and tons of fun. 
* The story is engaging and compelling. 
* The voice acting is excellent. 
* The visuals are beautiful. 
* The sound design and music are on point. 

Honestly, one of my favorite video game experiences of all time. 

10/10 - Highly Recommend

reviews.review_title: Frustrating return to game space bug
reviews.review_description: I bought this game because I’m a huge fan of this kind of puzzle games. First I occasionally ran into a ‘return to game space’ bug. Later on I ran into it every few minutes so I looked up if I was the only one but that isn’t the case I found. In other reviews I read that a bypass could be to reset the guardian but that’s no solution to this problem; Shadow point is the only game in which I encounter this problem and this almost made me lose interest in the game. Since the gameplay was so good and the dilemma’s and puzzles challenging I’m pleased I finally finished the game which is the only explanation I rewarded the game with 3 stars. If this bug wasn’t a really annoying part of the gameplay then I would have even rewarded Shadow Point with 5 stars. Therefor I would like to recommend the developers to fix this bug and not recommend the bypass about resetting the guardian, to score the reward Shadow Point deserves. There’s no fun in starting the game all over, again and again.

I really like this game....beautiful graphics, interesting story, but some of the puzzles are impossible to figure out without watching the walkthroughs on YouTube. There are a few glitches, but nothing really major. I definitely recommend it if you like puzzle/mystery type VR games, but be prepared to feel some frustration when trying to figure out certain chapters.
I’m really loving this one.

Just purchased an Oculus, the first time I have used any type of VR, 
I found this game to be a very very good starter game for people like me who are new to VR, and the other side of 50 lol,
Excellent gameplay really nice graphics, good storyline and the puzzles well thought out which really made you think about how to solve them,
I would highly recommend anyone of all ages to add this to they collection.
